entries.title: Hardware Accelerated GPU Scheduling - DirectX Developer Blog
entries.snippet: You may have noticed a mysterious new optional feature called appear in the advanced graphics settings page with the Windows 10 May 2020 update. The purpose of this blog is to give some background on this new feature and how we are introducing it. It is intended for folks curious about Windows internals. Remaining on the cutting edge of hardware innovation has always been a critical aspect of our graphics platform. Hardware Accelerated GPU Scheduling enables more efficient GPU scheduling between

entries.title: 4. Using Python on Windows — Python 3.14.3 documentation
entries.snippet: This document aims to give an overview of Windows-specific behaviour you should know about when using Python on Microsoft Windows. Unlike most Unix systems and services, Windows does not include a system supported installation of Python. Instead, Python can be obtained from a number of distributors, including directly from the CPython team. Each Python distribution will have its own benefits and drawbacks, however, consistency with other tools you are using is generally a worthwhile benefit. Bef

manual_reviews.notes: Manual review confirmed the exact quote in the validation proof matches the current Python Windows documentation wording about LongPathsEnabled.

Manual review confirmed Python's Windows documentation explicitly points readers to Enable Win32 long paths or LongPathsEnabled=1 in the FileSystem registry key. The record uses this page for the Python workflow context.

entries.title: Optimizations for windowed games in Windows 11 - Microsoft Support
entries.h1: Optimizations for windowed games in Windows 11
entries.snippet: Optimizations for windowed games improves gaming performance for DirectX 10 and DirectX 11 games running in windowed and borderless windowed modes. It transitions compatible games from the legacy blt‑model presentation to the newer flip‑model, reducing frame latency and enabling modern gaming features such as Auto HDR and variable refresh rate on supported displays.
